steps.input:
{"prompt":[{"role":"system","content":"You are a helpful coding agent that can view and edit files.

EDITING RULES:
1. ALWAYS use "view" before any edit to see the exact file content with line numbers.
2. For str_replace: copy old_str CHARACTER-FOR-CHARACTER from the view output. Even a single space difference will fail.
3. If old_str matches multiple locations, add more surrounding context to make it unique.
4. Prefer str_replace over insert/delete_lines for most edits — it's safer and more precise.

WORKFLOW:
- View the file first to understand context
- Copy the exact string you want to replace
- Make your edit
- View the file again to verify the change"},{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Hello how r u"}]}],"tools":[{"type":"function","name":"fileEditor","description":"A file editor tool for viewing and editing files.

COMMANDS:
- view         → Read a file with line numbers. Always do this before editing.
- create       → Create a new file with given content.
- delete       → Delete a file.
- str_replace  → Replace an exact string with new text. The old_str must match exactly

{"textParts":[{"id":"txt-0","text":"I'd be happy to call a tool! Could you please specify which tool you'd like me to use and what you'd like me to do with it?

I have access to:
- **`fileEditor`** - Can view, create, edit, or delete files
- **`listDir`** - Can list directory contents

For example, I could:
- View a specific file
- List the contents of a directory
- Create a new file
- Edit an existing file

What would you like me to do?"}],"reasoningParts":[{"id":"reasoning-0","text":"The user is asking me to "call a tool". This is a bit vague. They haven't specified which tool to call or what parameters to use. 

I have access to two tools:
1. `fileEditor` - for viewing and editing files
2. `listDir` - for listing directory contents

Since they haven't specified which tool or what to do with it, I should ask them to clarify what they want me to do. However, I could also make a reasonable interpretation - perhaps they want me to call one of the tools I have available to demonstrate how they work.

Let me ask for clarification about which tool they want me to call and what they want me to do with it."}],"toolCalls":[],"finishReason":{"unified":"stop","raw":"stop"},"usage":{"inputTokens":{},"outputTokens":{}}}

1. ALWAYS use "view" before any edit to see the exact file content with line numbers.
2. For str_replace: copy old_str CHARACTER-FOR-CHARACTER from the view output. Even a single space difference will fail.
3. If old_str matches multiple locations, add more surrounding context to make it unique.
4. Prefer str_replace over insert/delete_lines for most edits — it's safer and more precise.

  6 | import { strReplaceEditorTool } from './tools/file-editor-str-replace-based.ts';
  7 | import { listDirTool } from './tools/list-dir.ts';
  8 | 
  9 | export const agent = new ToolLoopAgent({
 10 |   model: wrappedLitellm('zai-org/glm-4.7-maas'),
 11 |   instructions: `You are a helpful coding agent that can view and edit files.
 12 | 
 13 | EDITING RULES:
 14 | 1. ALWAYS use "view" before any edit to see the exact file content with line numbers.
 15 | 2. For str_replace: copy old_str CHARACTER-FOR-CHARACTER from the view output. Even a single space difference will fail.
 16 | 3. If old_str matches multiple locations, add more surrounding context to make it unique.
 17 | 4. Prefer str_replace over insert/delete_lines for most edits — it's safer and more precise.
 18 | 
 19 | WORKFLOW:
 20 | - View the file first to understand context
 21 | - Copy the exact string you want to replace
 22 | - Make your edit
 23 | - View the file again to verify the change`,
 24 |   tools: {
 25 |     fileEditor: strReplaceEditorTool,
 26 |     listDir: listDirTool,
 27 |   },
 28 | });
 29 | 
 30 | // Persistent history that survives across turns
 31 | const messages: ModelMessage[] = [];
 32 | 
 33 | 
 34 | async function runWithPrompt(prompt: string) {
 35 |   messages.push({ role: 'user', content: prompt });
 36 |   const result = await agent.stream({ messages });
 37 | 
 38 |   let inReasoning = false;
 39 | 
 40 |   for await (const part of result.fullStream) {
 41 |     switch (part.type) {
 42 |       case 'reasoning-delta':
 43 |         inReasoning = true;
 44 |         process.stdout.write('\x1b[90m' + part.text + '\x1b[0m');
 45 |         break;
 46 |       case 'text-delta':
 47 |         if (inReasoning) {
 48 |           inReasoning = false;
 49 |           process.stdout.write('\n');
 50 |         }
 51 |         process.stdout.write(part.text);
 52 |         break;
 53 |       case 'tool-call':
 54 |         console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 55 |         console.log('Tool called:', part.toolName);
 56 |         const inputStr = JSON.stringify(part.input, null, 2) ?? 'undefined';
 57 |         console.log('Input:', inputStr.replace(/\n/g, '\n '));
 58 |         
 59 |         if (part.toolName === 'fileEditor' && (part.input as any).command === 'str_replace') {
 60 |           const red = '\x1b[31m';
 61 |           const green = '\x1b[32m';
 62 |           const reset = '\x1b[0m';
 63 |           console.log('\nDiff:');
 64 |           for (const line of ((part.input as any).old_str as string).split('\n')) {
 65 |             console.log(`${red}-${reset} ${line}`);
 66 |           }
 67 |           for (const line of (((part.input as any).new_str as string) ?? '').split('\n')) {
 68 |             console.log(`${green}+${reset} ${line}`);
 69 |           }
 70 |         }
 71 |         console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 72 |         break;
 73 |       case 'finish':
 74 |         console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 75 |         console.log('Finish reason:', part.finishReason);
 76 |         console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 77 |         break;
 78 |     }
 79 |   }
 80 | 
 81 |   const { messages: responseMessages } = await result.response;
 82 |   messages.push(...responseMessages);
 83 | }
 84 | 
 85 | 
 86 | const rl = readline.createInterface({ input, output });
 87 | 
 88 | console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 89 | console.log('Initial analysis complete. You can now ask follow-up questions.');
 90 | console.log('Type "exit" or "quit" to end the session.');
 91 | console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────\n');
 92 | 
 93 | let running = true;
 94 | 
 95 | while (running) {
 96 |   try {
 97 |     const answer = await rl.question('You: ');
 98 | 
 99 |     if (answer.toLowerCase() === 'exit' || answer.toLowerCase() === 'quit') {
100 |       console.log('Goodbye!');
101 |       running = false;
102 |     } else {
103 |       await runWithPrompt(answer);
104 |     }
105 |   } catch (error) {
106 |     console.error('\nError:', error);
107 |     running = false;
108 |   }
109 | }
110 | 
111 | rl.close();"}}]},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"reasoning","text":"Perfect! I found the file 'agent-2.ts' and successfully viewed its contents.   8 | 
  9 | export const agent = new ToolLoopAgent({
 10 |   model: wrappedLitellm('zai-org/glm-4.7-maas'),
 11 |   instructions: `You are a helpful coding agent that can view and edit files.
 12 | 
 13 | EDITING RULES:

 18 |

 26 |     listDir: listDirTool,
 27 |   },

 30 | // Persistent history that survives across turns
 31 | const messages: ModelMessage[] = [];
 32 | 
 33 | 
 34 | async function runWithPrompt(prompt: string) {
 35 |   messages.push({ role: 'user', content: prompt });
 36 |   const result = await agent.stream({ messages });
 37 | 
 38 |   let inReasoning = false;
 39 |

 41 |     switch (part.type) {
 42 |       case 'reasoning-delta':
 43 |         inReasoning = true;
 44 |         process.stdout.write('\x1b[90m' + part.text + '\x1b[0m');
 45 |         break;
 46 |       case 'text-delta':
 47 |         if (inReasoning) {
 48 |           inReasoning = false;
 49 |           process.stdout.write('\n');
 50 |         }
 51 |         process.stdout.write(part.text);
 52 |         break;
 53 |       case 'tool-call':
 54 |         console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 55 |         console.log('Tool called:', part.toolName);
 56 |         const inputStr = JSON.stringify(part.input, null, 2) ?? 'undefined';
 57 |         console.log('Input:', inputStr.replace(/\n/g, '\n '));
 58 |         
 59 |         if (part.toolName === 'fileEditor' && (part.input as any).command === 'str_replace') {
 60 |           const red = '\x1b[31m';
 61 |           const green = '\x1b[32m';
 62 |           const reset = '\x1b[0m';
 63 |           console.log('\nDiff:');
 64 |           for (const line of ((part.input as any).old_str as string).split('\n')) {
 65 |             console.log(`${red}-${reset} ${line}`);
 66 |           }
 67 |           for (const line of (((part.input as any).new_str as string) ?? '').split('\n')) {
 68 |             console.log(`${green}+${reset} ${line}`);
 69 |           }
 70 |         }
 71 |         console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 72 |         break;
 73 |       case 'finish':
 74 |         console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 75 |         console.log('Finish reason:', part.finishReason);
 76 |         console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 77 |         break;
 78 |     }
 79 |   }
 80 | 
 81 |   const { messages: responseMessages } = await result.response;
 82 |   messages.push(...responseMessages);
 83 | }
 84 | 
 85 | 
 86 | const rl = readline.createInterface({ input, output });
 87 | 
 88 | console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 89 | console.log('Initial analysis complete. You can now ask follow-up questions.');
 90 | console.log('Type "exit" or "quit" to end the session.');
 91 | console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────\n');
 92 | 
 93 | let running = true;

 95 | while (running) {

100 |       console.log('Goodbye!');
101 |       running = false;
102 |     } else {
103 |       await runWithPrompt(answer);
104 |     }
105 |   } catch (error) {

107 |     running = false;
108 |   }
109 | }
110 |

  9 | export const agent = new ToolLoopAgent({
 10 |   model: wrappedLitellm('zai-org/glm-4.7-maas'),

 32 | 
 33 | 
 34 | async function runWithPrompt(prompt: string) {

 54 |         console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');

 56 |         const inputStr = JSON.stringify(part.input, null, 2) ?? 'undefined';

 58 |

 60 |           const red = '\x1b[31m';

 62 |           const reset = '\x1b[0m';
 63 |           console.log('\nDiff:');
 64 |           for (const line of ((part.input as any).old_str as string).split('\n')) {
 65 |             console.log(`${red}-${reset} ${line}`);
 66 |           }
 67 |           for (const line of (((part.input as any).new_str as string) ?? '').split('\n')) {
 68 |             console.log(`${green}+${reset} ${line}`);
 69 |           }
 70 |         }
 71 |         console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 72 |         break;
 73 |       case 'finish':
 74 |         console.log('\n─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 75 |         console.log('Finish reason:', part.finishReason);
 76 |         console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────');
 77 |         break;
 78 |     }
 79 |   }
 80 | 
 81 |   const { messages: responseMessages } = await result.response;
 82 |   messages.push(...responseMessages);
 83 | }

 87 |

 89 | console.log('Initial analysis complete. You can now ask follow-up questions.');
 90 | console.log('Type "exit" or "quit" to end the session.');
 91 | console.log('─────────────────────────────────────────────\n');
 92 |

101 |       running = false;

107 |     running = false;
108 |   }
109 | }
110 |

 32 |
